metadata.title: SYNGO_CC_1023
metadata.comment:
Rationale: The paper uses immunogold-silver detection to study the subcellular localization of WLS in noradrenergic locus coeruleus neurons. Wls is a multi-pass transmembrane glycoprotein essential for Wnt cell signaling.

Figure 3 shows silver deposits in somatodendritic processes apposing a presynaptic, vesicle-filled terminal. Some deposits are associated with the plasma membrane. Since that a clear association with sub cellular organelles is not seen or mentioned, and the membrane association is not directly at the postsynaptic membrane, one can (only) use the above-mentioned data to demonstrate a post-synaptic localization of Wls.. Experimental description: 'Twelve adult male Sprague-Dawley rats (Harlan Sprague Dawley Inc., Indianapolis, IN, USA; 250-270g) housed two to three to a cage (20°C, 12-h light, 12-h dark cycle lights on 0700) were used in this study.'
